Overview
[
]

Mo Zhi, is an important supporting character in the novel. His true identity is a disciple of the overlord of Chu Yun Kingdom, a level-eight cultivation nation in Kun Xu. Possessing the cultivation level of a second step cultivator, he can perceive the laws of heaven and earth and wields destructive power. He once served as an envoy of the 
Cultivation Alliance
 stationed at 
Planet Suzaku
.

In a rainy night at a ruined temple in Zhao Kingdom, through a philosophical discourse on 
life and death,
 he helped Wang Lin break through the bottleneck of the Deity Transformation stage, subtly guiding him to comprehend the mysteries of the Extreme Realm. Later, he intervened as a mediator in key events such as the Vermilion Bird Tomb Battle. His actions are essentially manipulated by 
Tian Yunzi
, and he shares a hidden secret relationship as fellow apprentices of 
All-Seer
 with Wang Lin.

Rainy Night
[
]

When Mo Zhi encountered Wang Lin by chance on a rainy night at the border of Zhao Kingdom, he used the metaphor "fire as life, falling rain as human existence" to guide Wang Lin to a breakthrough in the Soul Transformation stage, achieving a complete realization of his conceptual realm. By introducing the philosophy of 
when the heart perishes, one forgets,
 Mo Zhi led Wang Lin to comprehend the Law of Life and Death, laying a crucial ideological foundation for his future cultivation.

Vermilion Bird Tomb Incident
[
]

As an envoy of the Cultivation Alliance, Mo Zhi intervened in the Vermilion Bird Tomb Battle (occurring 300 years after Wang Lin’s Soul Transformation). With his Second step cultivation, he forcibly suppressed conflicts among various factions. This action upheld the authority of the Cultivation Alliance while securing critical growth time for Wang Lin.

Vermilion Bird Tomb Battle intervention: Donghua Episode 102

Relationship
[
]

Wang Lin
[
]

On the path of cultivation, Mo Zhi and Wang Lin influence and support each other. Mo Zhi’s guidance enabled Wang Lin to achieve rapid progress in a short time, laying a solid foundation for his future standing in the cultivation world.
While appearing as an ordinary fellow cultivator, Mo Zhi was secretly acting as Wang Lin’s protector under Tian Yunzi’s instructions. In later events, he represented their sect to present grand gifts congratulating Wang Lin on becoming the sixth Vermilion Bird Holy Emperor. A millennium later, he tried to persuade Wang Lin to abandon reviving Li Muwan and pursued the Boundary Compass.

With Tian Yunzi
[
]

As a disciple of one of Tian Yunzi’s three thousand avatars, all of Mo Zhi’s actions serve his master’s grand scheme. This unique relationship grants Mo Zhi immense power but also binds him to a fate of being manipulated.

Abilities
[
]

Mo Zhi possesses a spiritual energy reserve capable of destroying heaven and earth. Ordinary spells, when cast by him, produce transformative effects. During the Vermilion Bird Tomb Incident, his overwhelming presence directly nullified the combined attacks of multiple Ascendant-stage cultivators.

As a messenger of the cultivation alliance on the planet of Suzaku, it is estimated that his cultivation level is above the strongest on the planet Suzaku, the son of Suzaku himself who is at the peak of the late stage of the ascendant, estimates that his combat abilities are above that

He can see through Wang Lin’s primary and secondary avatars and the secrets of the Extreme Realm. His understanding of the Law of Life and Death reaches the natural state of 
rain is born in the heavens, dies on the earth
. This ability makes him the Cultivation Alliance’s top choice for handling complex matters.

Influenced by Tian Yunzi, Mo Zhi possesses exceptional long-term planning capabilities. During the rainy night discourse, he foresaw Wang Lin’s growth trajectory over a millennium. Each of his appearances precisely aligns with critical junctures in Wang Lin’s cultivation, forming a thousand-year protective cycle.

Intent Realm
[
]

Forgetfulness Intent realm
 showed in episode 62
